title: Steady-state phases in long-range measurement-only quantum circuits
abstract: Measurements can drive quantum many-body systems into nontrivial steady states and induce interesting dynamical phase transitions, rendering measurement-only quantum circuits a useful platform for exploring quantum many-body phases beyond those of equilibrium Hamiltonian systems. Here we study a class of long-range measurement-only quantum circuits with competing two-qubit and three-qubit measurements. We demonstrate that these circuits exhibit rich steady-state structure and uncover a strong influence of the measurement range on the resulting phases. In particular, states with symmetry-protected topological (SPT) order can emerge with sufficiently short-range measurements beyond the nearest-neighbor limit. These states feature robust topological edge modes, which can also be detected from circuit dynamics. With longer-range measurements, an extended parameter regime emerges in which conventional order parameters are suppressed while spatial correlations remain nontrivial. Moreover, we show that in this circuit model sufficiently long-range measurements can produce significant entanglement with scaling beyond an area law despite the absence of any unitary evolution.
summary: The paper explores how the range of measurements in a 1D quantum circuit influences steady-state phases like SPT and SSB. It finds that long-range measurements can destroy topological order and induce a regime with entanglement scaling that exceeds the area law. This provides new insights into how non-local monitoring can be used to control many-body quantum states.
main_problem: Investigating how long-range connectivity and nonlocality affect the steady-state phase structure, entanglement scaling, and competition between topological and symmetry-breaking orders in measurement-only quantum circuits.
main_result: The study uncovers a rich phase diagram where SPT order persists for short-range measurements but is destabilized by long-range interactions, and identifies an intermediate regime with power-law correlations and entanglement that exceeds the area law.
method: Numerical simulations using the stabilizer formalism and Gottesman-Knill theorem to evolve Clifford circuits, including finite-size scaling and edge-probe protocols.
model_or_system: A 1D measurement-only quantum circuit consisting of competing two-qubit Ising-type measurements and three-qubit cluster measurements with a power-law distribution for the measurement range.
key_observables: SSB order parameter (O_SSB), SPT order parameter (O_SPT), string correlations, spin-spin correlations, half-chain entanglement entropy, and generalized topological entanglement entropy.
important_parameters: Measurement probability (p_ZZ), measurement range parameter (alpha), and system size (L).
main_assumptions: The dynamics are restricted to the stabilizer formalism (Clifford operations), and the system is studied in the 1D limit.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the circuit architecture; Figure 2 presents the steady-state phase diagram; Figures 9 and 10 show the finite-size scaling of SPT and SSB order parameters.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the long-range measurement model, presents the steady-state phase diagram, analyzes the stability of SPT and SSB phases, explores entanglement scaling and correlation decay, and concludes with discussions on universality and higher-dimensional extensions.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant for researchers in open quantum systems and many-body dynamics as it demonstrates how non-unitary, non-local measurements can engineer exotic phases and entanglement scaling beyond the area law without any unitary evolution.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic of the long-range measurement-only quan- tum circuit. The circuit dynamics is driven by two types of measurements, the three-qubit cluster measurements and the ZZ measurements on two qubits.
FIG. 2. Steady-state phase diagram of the long-range measurement-only circuit, characterized by max(OSSB, OSPT) (color bar).

FIG. 3. Purification dynamics. The time-evolution of the full- system entanglement entropy S is plotted for two different ranges of the ZZ measurements, α = 1 (left) and α = 3 (right). Different lines represent the dynamics obtained at various different measurement probabilities pZZ.

FIG. 4. Edge responses at the steady state when a bound- ary measurement perturbation is applied with probability pb, in a circuit with the two-qubit measurements of α = 1 (left) and α = 3 (right). Different colors represent the results ob- tained at different two-qubit measurement probabilities, rang- ing from pZZ = 0 to pZZ = 1.
FIG. 7. Decay of string correlations CSPT (left) and con- nected spin-spin correlations CZZ (right), plotted in double logarithmic scale.
